Name: SS Yongala Wreck
Description: One of the worlds best wreck dives, the SS Yongala sank in 1911 and is now an artificial reef teeming with marine life. The 109-meter vessel attracts massive schools of fish and pelagic species.
Marine Life: Bull sharks, eagle rays, sea snakes, giant grouper, trevally, barracuda
Hazards: Strong currents, penetration hazards, marine life encounters
Facilities: Day boat access, advanced certification required

Name: Julian Rocks
Description: A volcanic rock formation 2.5km offshore from Byron Bay, offering diverse marine life and excellent underwater photography opportunities. Protected marine park with year-round diving.
Marine Life: Grey nurse sharks, leopard sharks, turtles, rays, tropical fish
Facilities: Multiple dive operators, shore dive possible

Name: Julian Rocks – Cod Hole
Description: Swim-through with large cod and pelagics.

Name: Julian Rocks – The Nursery
Description: Sheltered site with turtles, rays and wobbegongs.

Name: Julian Rocks – Hugo's Trench
Description: Narrow trench with reef sharks and strong visibility.

Name: Julian Rocks Marine Sanctuary
Description: A world-class dive site within the Cape Byron Marine Park, home to over 1,000 marine species.
Marine Life: Manta rays, leopard sharks, grey nurse sharks, turtles, reef fish

Name: North Bondi Rocks
Description: Sheltered site with rocky outcrops and marine life near the rock pool.
Nearby Runoff: Bondi Beach stormwater drain approximately 600m north; large urban catchment from Bondi, Bellevue Hill and surrounds
Viz Triggers: Northerly swells churn sand; strong easterly swell drops viz to under 3m; rain events reduce viz by 50-60% for 2-3 days
Viz Seasonal: Summer: 8-12m; Autumn best (10-15m); Winter: 6-10m; Spring: 5-8m (plankton blooms)

Name: North Bondi Bommie
Description: Rocky bommie with good fish life and occasional pelagics.
Nearby Runoff: Same Bondi Beach stormwater as North Bondi Rocks - large urban catchment from Bondi and surrounds
Viz Triggers: Same as North Bondi Rocks - northerly swells and rain events reduce viz; bommie surge from any easterly swell
Viz Seasonal: Same as North Bondi Rocks: Summer 8-12m, Autumn best (10-15m), Winter 6-10m, Spring 5-8m
